reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Phase 03 claims ComputeForkDigest bypasses blob parameter incorporation, but this is by design and not exploitable. Code verification: ComputeForkDigest (signing_root.go:304-310) is a legacy implementation used only by the bootnode tool for manual configuration. All production validator/P2P code uses params.ForkDigest(epoch) → networkSchedule.ForEpoch(epoch).ForkDigest, which correctly implements EIP-7892's XOR mechanism (config.go:576-590). The attack scenario is impossible because: (1) bootnode advertises digest computed via ComputeForkDigest (no blob params), (2) validators validate using network schedule digest (WITH blob params via XOR), (3) these digests will NOT match for networks with different blob configs, so validators reject incompatible bootnodes. The two-tier system (manual bootnode vs automated schedule) is intentional - bootnodes are discovery nodes that advertise a specific fork version, while validators use epoch-based lookups. Spec reference confirms compute_fork_digest SHOULD incorporate blob params (SG-003 line 8-10), which the network schedule correctly implements. No exploitable path exists.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fulu SG-003 (compute_fork_digest) requires XOR with hash(epoch||max_blobs) per EIP-7892. Network schedule implements this correctly; bootnode uses legacy direct call but validators reject mismatched digests.
